Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze an event log of an Insurance Claims Processing process identify cases with performance issues long durations and deduce the root causes based on event level attributes Resource Region Complexity Finally propose explanations and mitigation strategies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Event Log Process Steps Submit Claim Evaluate Claim Request Additional Documents optional Approve Claim Pay Claim Close Claim Attributes Resource Region Complexity Cases 2001 2002 2003 2004 2005

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Calculate Case Durations Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 End 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 5 hours Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 End 2024 04 02 11 00 Duration 25 9 hours Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 End 2024 04 03 09 30 Duration 48 3 hours Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 End 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 4 hours Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 End 2024 04 04 14 30 Duration 77 1 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identify Performance Issues Long Durations Cases 2001 and 2004 are very fast 1 5 hours They are both Low complexity Case 2002 takes 26 hours It is Medium complexity Case 2003 takes 48 hours It is High complexity Case 2005 takes 77 hours It is High complexity Conclusion Cases 2002 2003 and 2005 have significant performance issues with 2005 being the worst

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity Low 2001 2004 1 5 hours No Request Additional Documents step Medium 2002 26 hours 1 Request Additional Documents step High 2003 2005 48 to 77 hours Multiple Request Additional Documents steps 2 for 2003 3 for 2005 Observation High complexity strongly correlates with longer durations primarily due to the multiple iterations of requesting additional documents

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Region A 2001 2003 1 5 hrs Low 48 3 hrs High Region B 2002 2004 2005 25 9 hrs Medium 1 4 hrs Low 77 1 hrs High Observation Comparing similar complexities Low A 1 5 vs B 1 4 Negligible difference High A 48 3 vs B 77 1 Region B takes significantly longer for High complexity Conclusion Region B seems to struggle more with High complexity claims compared to Region A

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource Lets look at who handles the Request Additional Documents and Evaluate Claim steps as these are where the bottlenecks occur

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Adjuster Lisa Region B Handles 2002 Medium 1 request 2004 Low 0 requests 2005 High 3 requests Adjuster Mike Region A Handles 2001 Low 0 requests 2003 High 2 requests Managers Manager Ann Approves 2001 15 mins after eval 2002 10 00 AM next day 2004 30 mins after eval Manager Bill Approves 2003 9 hours after last request 2005 17 hours after last request Finance Alan Pays 2001 15 mins 2003 16 hours after approval Finance Carl Pays 2002 45 mins 2004 30 mins 2005 4 hours after approval

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deep Dive into the bottleneck Request Additional Documents Case 2003 Region A Adjuster Mike Requests at 11 00 and 17 00 on Day 1 Approved Day 2 at 16 00 Case 2005 Region B Adjuster Lisa Requests at 11 30 Day 1 17 00 Day 2 15 00 Day 3 Approved Day 4 at 10 00 Observation Adjuster Lisa in Region B takes much longer to resolve the additional document requests for high complexity claims compared to Adjuster Mike in Region A

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets look at the time between the last Request Additional Documents and Approve Claim 2003 Last request 04 01 17 00 Approved 04 02 16 00 23 hours 2005 Last request 04 03 15 00 Approved 04 04 10 00 19 hours Wait what about the time between requests 2003 11 00 to 17 00 6 hours 2005 11 30 Day 1 to 17 00 Day 2 29 5 hours 17 00 Day 2 to 15 00 Day 3 22 hours Observation Adjuster Lisa Region B has massive gaps between requesting additional documents for high complexity claims suggesting she might be overloaded waiting on the customer or inefficient in following up Adjuster Mike Region A had a 6 hour gap

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait what about the time between requests

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings and Formulate Explanations Root Cause 1 Complexity and Document Requests High complexity claims require multiple rounds of Request Additional Documents This is the primary driver of long durations The back and forth adds days to the process Root Cause 2 Region B Performance specifically with High Complexity Region B takes 29 hours longer than Region A for high complexity Why Look at the resources Root Cause 3 Resource Bottlenecks Adjuster Lisa and Manager Bill Adjuster Lisa Region B handles the high complexity claim 2005 and leaves huge gaps 29 5 hrs 22 hrs between document requests Adjuster Mike Region A only leaves a 6 hour gap Manager Bill who handles high complexity approvals in both regions takes a long time to approve 9 to 17 hours whereas Manager Ann low medium takes minutes to hours This suggests Manager Bill might be a bottleneck for complex approvals or complex approvals just inherently take longer However the biggest delay is clearly in the document request phase

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Develop Mitigation Strategies For Complexity Document Requests Implement better upfront collection of documents Use a checklist for high complexity claims so adjusters know exactly what to request the first time reducing the need for multiple rounds For Region B Adjuster Lisa Investigate why there are such long gaps between document requests Is it a workload issue Is she waiting on the customer and not following up Provide training or redistribute workload Maybe Adjuster Lisa is newer or less experienced with high complexity claims compared to Adjuster Mike For Manager Bill Assess why high complexity approvals sit for 9 to 17 hours Is there a queue Does he need support Could some of these approvals be delegated or automated for certain thresholds

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Structure the Output Introduction Briefly state the objective Part 1 Identify Cases with Performance Issues List the cases and their durations clearly Highlight the outliers Part 2 Root Cause Analysis Attributes Break down by Complexity Region and Resource Use specific data points from the log to back up claims Part 3 Proposed Explanations and Mitigation Strategies Provide actionable business advice based on the analysis

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refine the Content Self Correction during drafting Check Did I calculate durations correctly 2001 09 00 to 10 30 1 5h 2002 09 05 to 11 00 next day 26h 2003 09 10 to 09 30 two days later 48h 2004 09 20 to 10 45 1 25h 2005 09 25 to 14 30 three days later 77h Check Is the resource analysis fair Yes Adjuster Lisas gaps in Case 2005 are glaring almost a full day between requests Adjuster Mikes gap in Case 2003 is only 6 hours This is a strong indicator of a resource specific issue in Region B Clarity Make sure the distinction between the inherent delay of high complexity multiple document requests and the operational delay long gaps between requests is clear

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Final Polish Ensure professional tone clear headings and logical flow Proceed to generate output based on these structured thoughts
